summary: Bright, cleaned‑up soundboard capture of a late‑'73 San Diego show highlighted by a stellar China Cat → Rider sequence and an epic Other One sandwich.
review: The recording is a well‑fixed SBD that sounds noticeably cleaner and brighter than earlier copies, with only minor pitch‑correction quirks. Musically the concert is praised for its second‑set fireworks – a unique China Cat > Rider, a triple‑sandwich The Other One, tight Eyes of the World, and a standout Goin' Down the Road – even if the band sounds a touch tired in places. Overall fans regard it as one of the better ’73 performances.

ai_rating.rationale: All four reviewers gave five stars and highlighted both excellent musical moments and a cleaned‑up soundboard, outweighing minor fatigue comments.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Cleaned up version is brighter and clearer; fixes include pitch correction (−1% to −3% on various tracks) and channel swap. Some cuts are patched from audience sources, but overall the SBD is very listenable.

show_quality.setlist_flow: Second set is hailed as one of the year's best, with seamless transitions and a high‑energy sandwich of The Other One; first set has solid highlights but occasional average moments.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Angular, almost '76‑style playing on China Cat; his lead work on Eyes of the World is described as "smoking".
band_member_comments.Phil: Bass lines in The Other One are noted for their strength alongside Jerry and Bobby.
band_member_comments.Bob: Vocals praised throughout – especially on Black Throated Wind, Truckin' and Saturday Nite – with “cat‑calling” highlighted.
band_member_comments.Keys: Keith’s playing shines on Brown‑Eyed Women and adds texture to the second‑set jams.
